papers.title: SEGUE-2 Limits on Metal-Rich Old-Population Hypervelocity Stars In the Galactic Halo
papers.author_names: Juna A. Kollmeier Andrew Gould Constance Rockosi Timothy C. Beers Gillian Knapp Jennifer A. Johnson Heather Morrison Paul Harding Young Sun Lee Benjamin A. Weaver

abstract.text: We present new limits on the ejection of metal-rich old-population hypervelocity stars from the Galactic center (GC) as probed by the SEGUE-2 survey. Our limits are a factor of 3-10 more stringent than previously reported, depending on stellar type. Compared to the known population of B-star ejectees, there can be no more than 30 times more metal-rich old-population F/G stars ejected from the GC. Because B stars comprise a tiny fraction of a normal stellar population, this places significant limits on a combination of the GC mass function and the ejection mechanism for hypervelocity stars. In the presence of a normal GC mass function, our results require an ejection mechanism that is about 5.5 times more efficient at ejecting B-stars compared to low-mass F/G stars.

brief.tldr: [astronomical observational study / survey analysis] SEGUE-2 presents stringent new upper limits on the ejection of metal-rich old-population (F/G-type) hypervelocity stars from the Galactic center, finding at most 30 times more such stars than B-star ejectees. These limits imply a highly non-uniform ejection efficiency—5.5 times more efficient for B-stars than for low-mass F/G stars—under a normal stellar mass function, constraining both the GC mass function and the physical mechanisms responsible for hypervelocity star ejection.

source_fields.title: SEGUE-2 Limits on Metal-Rich Old-Population Hypervelocity Stars In the Galactic Halo

source_fields.sections_text: Sample: The sample consists of a subset of stars selected from the SEGUE-2 survey based on stringent criteria involving galactic latitude, photometric metallicity, proper motion, and space velocity to identify high-metallicity stars likely ejected from the Galactic Center. Results: The experiment found no metal-rich old-population hypervelocity stars in the SEGUE-2 survey, with a sensitivity of approximately 2.3 Myr⁻¹ for stars ejected at 500 km s⁻¹ and a completeness factor of ~50% due to photometric and proper-motion errors, suggesting either a true absence of such stars or overly stringent selection criteria. Introduction: This paper introduces a targeted search within SEGUE-2 to detect metal-rich old-population hypervelocity stars, addressing the critical gap in the kinematic census of the Galactic halo by providing tighter constraints on the ejection of old stars from the Galactic center. Discussion and Conclusions: No metal-rich old-population hypervelocity stars were detected in the SEGUE-2 survey, placing new limits on the ejection of such stars from the Galactic Center and implying either a top-heavy mass function, a strong preference for high-mass star ejection (1:5.5), or a sub-solar metallicity of ejected stars.

abstract: We present new limits on the ejection of metal-rich old-population hypervelocity stars from the Galactic center (GC) as probed by the SEGUE-2 survey.
Compared to the known population of B-star ejectees, there can be no more than 30 times more metal-rich old-population F/G stars ejected from the GC.
Because B stars comprise a tiny fraction of a normal stellar population, this places significant limits on a combination of the GC mass function and the ejection mechanism for hypervelocity stars.
In the presence of a normal GC mass function, our results require an ejection mechanism that is about 5.5 times more efficient at ejecting B-stars compared to low-mass F/G stars.
title: SEGUE-2 Limits on Metal-Rich Old-Population Hypervelocity Stars In the Galactic Halo

catalog_assessment.evidence: Paper analyzes SEGUE-2 survey data to place limits on hypervelocity star populations, implying object-level observational data was used for analysis
